title: task
description: python/src/lichen/sim/node_server.py:66 and renode_server.py:59 - read_message() unpacks a 4-byte length from the wire but does not validate it against MAX_PAYLOAD_LENGTH (65535). A malicious client can send length=0xFFFFFFFF, causing the server to attempt reading 4GB of data, leading to memory exhaustion or indefinite blocking. The protocol.py module defines MAX_PAYLOAD_LENGTH but it is only enforced at encoding time, not decoding. Fix: Add validation after unpacking length to reject values > MAX_PAYLOAD_LENGTH.
close_reason: Fixed: Added MAX_PAYLOAD_LENGTH validation to renode_server.py _read_message(). The node_server.py already had this validation at lines 74-76; applied same pattern to renode_server.py to prevent memory exhaustion from malicious oversized length values.
